scenarios.scenario_name: API misuse returns Err not panic

steps.step_text: the client attempts an invalid API operation
the operation returns an Err result

scenarios.scenario_name: Malformed inbound packet is dropped without panic

steps.step_text: the server receives a malformed packet

scenarios.scenario_name: Duplicate replication messages do not panic

steps.step_text: a connected client with replicated entities

scenarios.scenario_name: Protocol mismatch produces ProtocolMismatch rejection

steps.step_text: a server with protocol version A
a client with protocol version B
the connection is rejected with ProtocolMismatch

scenarios.scenario_name: Identical inputs produce identical outputs

steps.step_text: a test scenario with deterministic time
the same API call sequence is executed twice
the event emission order is identical both times
the entity spawn order is identical both times

scenarios.scenario_name: Same-tick scope operations resolve deterministically

steps.step_text: multiple scope operations queued for the same tick
the final scope state reflects the last API call order

scenarios.scenario_name: Multiple commands for same tick apply in receipt order

steps.step_text: a server receiving multiple commands for the same tick
commands are applied in receipt order

scenarios.scenario_name: Reconnecting client receives fresh entity spawns

steps.step_text: a client that was previously connected
it receives fresh entity spawns for all in-scope entities
no prior session state is retained

scenarios.scenario_name: Server observes ConnectEvent when client connects
Client observes ConnectEvent when connected
Client observes DisconnectEvent after disconnect
DisconnectEvent occurs only after ConnectEvent on server

steps.step_text: the server observed ConnectEvent before DisconnectEvent

scenarios.scenario_name: DisconnectEvent occurs only after ConnectEvent on client

steps.step_text: the client observed ConnectEvent before DisconnectEvent

scenarios.scenario_name: Server observes DisconnectEvent when client disconnects
Connected client count decreases after disconnect
Server can connect multiple clients
Server can disconnect one of multiple clients
Client is connected after successful connection
Client is not connected after disconnect
Server observes AuthEvent before ConnectEvent

steps.step_text: a server is running with auth required
the server observes AuthEvent before ConnectEvent

scenarios.scenario_name: Rejected client observes RejectEvent not ConnectEvent

steps.step_text: a server is running with auth required
a client attempts to connect but is rejected
the client does not observe ConnectEvent
the client does not observe DisconnectEvent

scenarios.scenario_name: Server full event ordering with disconnect

steps.step_text: a server is running with auth required
the server observes DisconnectEvent after ConnectEvent

scenarios.scenario_name: Server starts and accepts a connecting client
Server can disconnect a client
Multiple clients can connect to server
Server tracks client count accurately
Connecting client is in connected state
Disconnecting client is no longer connected
Server and client observe connect events
Server and client observe disconnect events
Event ordering is correct on disconnect

steps.step_text: the server observed ConnectEvent before DisconnectEvent
the client observed ConnectEvent before DisconnectEvent

steps.step_text: the server sends a packet within the MTU limit

scenarios.scenario_name: Client can send packet within MTU limit

steps.step_text: the client sends a packet within the MTU limit

scenarios.scenario_name: Server rejects outbound packet exceeding MTU

steps.step_text: the server attempts to send a packet exceeding MTU
the operation returns an Err result
the transport adapter is not called

scenarios.scenario_name: Client rejects outbound packet exceeding MTU

steps.step_text: the client attempts to send a packet exceeding MTU
the operation returns an Err result
the transport adapter is not called

steps.step_text: the server receives a packet exceeding MTU

scenarios.scenario_name: Client drops inbound packet exceeding MTU

steps.step_text: the client receives a packet exceeding MTU

steps.step_text: the server receives a malformed packet

scenarios.scenario_name: Client drops malformed inbound packet

steps.step_text: the client receives a malformed packet

scenarios.scenario_name: Server tolerates packet loss

steps.step_text: packets from the client are dropped by the transport

scenarios.scenario_name: Client tolerates packet loss

steps.step_text: packets from the server are dropped by the transport

scenarios.scenario_name: Server tolerates duplicate packets

steps.step_text: the server handles them without panic

scenarios.scenario_name: Client tolerates duplicate packets

steps.step_text: the client handles them without panic

scenarios.scenario_name: Server tolerates reordered packets

steps.step_text: the server receives packets in a different order than sent
the server handles them without panic

scenarios.scenario_name: Client tolerates reordered packets

steps.step_text: the client receives packets in a different order than sent
the client handles them without panic

scenarios.scenario_name: Application behavior is identical across transport types

steps.step_text: multiple transport adapters with different quality characteristics
the same application logic runs on each transport
